title: Dropdown Async Pagination
summary: Prior-art survey (pagination literature, the WAI-ARIA APG Feed pattern, React Aria useAsyncList, open MUI/Fluent/SAP/Radix feature-requests) grounding backlog #018's async-pagination forks for the dropdown family. The load-bearing finding: the item is largely overtaken by work landed since it was filed — cursor-vs-offset already lives in the Custom Pagination protocol, and windowing+async is a composition of Windowed Collection (dataSource:infinite) over Loader (filtering/loadingMore), which the autocomplete member already declares. So no net-new contract; the dropdown composes the three existing standards. The genuinely-open residue is three family-specific gaps: wire Collection Operations advance:manual|auto onto autocomplete (default auto + manual fallback), keep the family forward-only (load-earlier is a Feed concern), and adopt the Feed aria-setsize="-1" + aria-busy rule for the unknown-total case. No native async combobox pagination exists.
